## ⚠️ Diabetes Cross-Reference Note
You correctly observed that every DR patient should have a co-occurring diabetes diagnosis. Investigation reveals this is **structurally enforced by ICD-10-CM coding itself**: every DR code on this server is a child of the E08–E13 (Diabetes Mellitus) chapter — e.g., `E11.359 = "Type 2 diabetes mellitus with proliferative diabetic retinopathy"`. The diabetes diagnosis **is the DR diagnosis**. Likewise all SNOMED DR terms include "due to diabetes mellitus" in their fully specified name. Querying for a *separate* standalone diabetes condition (E11.9, SNOMED 44054006) returned **0 records** — the server does not double-code diabetes as a separate problem-list entry when the complication code already encodes it. ✅ No additional `_has` filter is needed; diabetes is guaranteed by virtue of the DR code being found.
